items.definition_en: Rain is water that falls from clouds.
items.example_en: We forgot our umbrella and got wet in the rain.

items.definition_en: Snow is soft white pieces of frozen water that fall from the sky.
items.example_en: The children are playing in the snow.

items.definition_en: Hail is small hard balls of ice that fall from the sky.
items.example_en: Hail damaged the car last night.

items.definition_en: Drizzle is light rain with very small drops.
items.example_en: There was a drizzle all morning.

items.definition_en: A shower is a short period of rain.
items.example_en: We had a quick shower in the afternoon.

items.definition_en: A downpour is a very heavy rain.
items.example_en: The downpour made the streets flood.

items.definition_en: Sleet is a mix of rain and snow.
items.example_en: Sleet made the roads slippery today.

items.definition_en: Mist is a light fog often mixed with small drops of water.
items.example_en: Mist covered the field early in the morning.

items.definition_en: A sprinkle is a small amount of rain.
items.example_en: There was only a sprinkle, so we kept walking.

items.definition_en: A flurry is a sudden short period of light snow.
items.example_en: We saw a flurry while driving to work.

items.definition_en: Frost is a thin layer of ice on surfaces when it is cold.
items.example_en: There was frost on the windows this morning.

items.definition_en: A rainstorm is a weather event with a lot of rain and wind.
items.example_en: The rainstorm kept us inside all day.

items.definition_en: An ice pellet is a small hard piece of ice falling from the sky.
items.example_en: Ice pellets hit the roof during the evening.

items.definition_en: Dew is small drops of water found on grass in the morning.
items.example_en: Morning dew made the grass wet.

items.definition_en: Freezing rain is rain that turns to ice when it hits the ground.
items.example_en: Driving is dangerous when there is freezing rain.

items.definition_en: Rainfall is the amount of rain that falls in a period.
items.example_en: This month we had a lot of rainfall.

items.definition_en: A raindrop is a single drop of rain.
items.example_en: A raindrop hit my face as I walked outside.

items.definition_en: A snowflake is a single piece of snow in a unique shape.
items.example_en: Each snowflake looks a little different.

items.definition_en: A blizzard is a very strong snowstorm with wind.
items.example_en: The blizzard closed many roads last winter.

items.definition_en: A cloudburst is a sudden very heavy rain.
items.example_en: A cloudburst surprised everyone at the picnic.
